stem: A fuel pump fills a storage tank at a constant rate of 12 gallons per minute. How many liters will the pump deliver in 45 minutes? (1 gallon = 3.785 liters)
explanation:
Step 1: Find the total gallons pumped in 45 minutes.
$$12 \text{ gal/min} \times 45 \text{ min} = 540 \text{ gallons}$$

Step 2: Convert gallons to liters using the given conversion factor.
$$540 \text{ gal} \times \frac{3.785 \text{ L}}{1 \text{ gal}} = 2{,}043.9 \text{ L} \approx 2{,}044 \text{ liters}$$

The pump delivers approximately **2,044 liters** in 45 minutes.

distractorRationale.A: Inverted conversion: student divides by 3.785 instead of multiplying. $540 \div 3.785 \approx 143$ liters. This error treats the conversion factor upside-down, dividing when the factor should be multiplied.
distractorRationale.B: Stops one step early: student correctly computes $12 \times 45 = 540$ gallons but reports gallons as the final answer without converting to liters.
distractorRationale.D: Uses wrong conversion factor: student substitutes $1 \text{ gal} = 4 \text{ L}$ (a rounded approximation) instead of the given 3.785 L/gal. $540 \times 4 = 2{,}160$ liters.
